codeReview.summary: Solid implementation covering all feature requirements: vera stats (file/chunk/size/language), vera config (show/get/set), --json for all commands, --help with examples, --version from Cargo.toml, exit code conventions, actionable errors, and pipe-friendly stdout/stderr separation. Code was well-refactored from monolithic main.rs into focused command modules (all under 500-line budget). Test coverage includes 7 CLI parsing tests and 2 core stats tests. All 356 tests pass with clean clippy/fmt.

issues.description: process::exit(1) called directly inside config::run() (and similarly in other command modules) instead of returning an Err that main() handles. This makes these code paths untestable in unit tests since process::exit terminates the process. Consider returning typed errors and let main() handle exit codes.

main.rs is 479 lines (above 300-line soft target). ~200 lines are tests. The refactoring was good — moved command logic to modules — but the clap struct definitions + tests together push the file size up. Could move tests to a separate tests/ module in the future.
The --help for 'vera config' lists 11 configuration keys, but get_config_value() supports 12 keys (indexing.default_excludes is missing from the help text). Users could miss this discoverable key.

When --json is used with 'vera config get', the output is a raw JSON value (e.g., 10) rather than a wrapped object with key/value. While valid JSON, this differs from the object-wrapped format used by 'config set --json' and may surprise API consumers expecting consistent envelope structure.

compute_dir_size() follows symlinks (uses is_dir/is_file which follow symlinks). If .vera/ ever contains symlinks, this could traverse outside the index directory or double-count files. Low risk since .vera/ is generated by Vera, but worth noting.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Multiple command modules (config.rs, search.rs, index.rs, update.rs, stats.rs) call process::exit(1) directly in non-main functions rather than propagating errors to main(). AGENTS.md says 'Return actionable errors with context' but doesn't explicitly say whether process::exit() is acceptable in command handlers vs returning Result. Could clarify the convention.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: config.rs:28, config.rs:44, config.rs:66, config.rs:87, config.rs:97, search.rs:35, search.rs:100, search.rs:111, index.rs:17, index.rs:24, stats.rs:13, update.rs:16, update.rs:23, update.rs:46 — all use process::exit(1) instead of returning Err

sharedStateObservations.observation: The error message format (Error: <problem>\nHint: <suggestion>) is now a de-facto pattern used consistently across all CLI commands. This pattern is not documented in AGENTS.md as a convention, though it's very consistent in this implementation. Could be formalized.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: config.rs uses 'Error: ...' + 'Hint: ...' pattern, index.rs same, search.rs same, update.rs same, stats.rs same. All actionable error messages follow this format.

summary: The cli-completeness feature passes review. All 8 expected behaviors are implemented: vera stats with file/chunk/size/language data, vera config with show/get/set, --json flag producing valid JSON on all output commands, comprehensive --help with examples for all commands, --version matching Cargo.toml, non-zero exit codes for all errors with actionable messages, and pipe-friendly stdout/stderr separation. The code was well-structured into focused command modules, respects size budgets (all files under 500 lines), and includes 9 new tests. The only notable limitation (config set not persisting) is documented in the code and handoff. 5 non-blocking issues identified: process::exit() in non-main code, main.rs above 300-line soft target, one missing config key in help text, inconsistent JSON envelope for config get, and symlink-following in dir size calculation.
